summary.p: #100. a) Tiruvālantuṟai Mahādeva temple; b) on the wall section immediately on the northern side of the Liṅgodbhavamūrti, on the western façade; c) personally located and read in situ; d) ARE 1926, no. 232; SII 13, no. 209; e) 10th regnal year of Kōvirājakesarivarman; f) king difficult to identify; g) inscription not read with anyone.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī
kovirājakeca
sarivanmaṟkku yā
ṇṭu 10 Āvatu
kuṉṟakkūṟṟattu
{space}brahmadeya
ciṟupaḻuvūrt tiruvā
lantuṟaimahādevarku
Ivvūr maṉṟāṭi nilaiya
ṉ pukaḻaṉ vaitta [Ā]ṭu
60 Ivaṟṟuḷp paka
l viḷakkeriya nica
tam [Ā]ḻākku neykku 40
5 Āṭu nikki niṉṟa Ā
ṭu 15 m Uttaramayaṉa
m [paṟṟu]sa[ṅ]kirānti nāṉṉ
ṟāṭiyaruḷa vaitta canay
15 [sa]yThese two glyphs may be an abbreviation for a measure, perhapsnāḻi. See also #97. Itu panmā
heśvara ra[kṣai]

Fortune! Prosperity! This is the 10th year of Kōvirājakesarivarman. For Mahādeva of Tiruvālantuṟai of Ciṟupaḻuvūr, a brahmadeya of Kuṉṟakkūṟṟam, Nilaiyaṉ Pukaḻaṉ, a shepherd (maṉṟāṭi) of this village (ivvūr), placed (vaitta) 60 goats (āṭu). In these (ivaṟṟuḷ), having removed (nīkki) 45 goats (āṭu) for one uḻakku (āḻakku > uḻakku) of ghee (neykku) every day (nicatam) to burn (eriya) a day-lamp (pakal viḷakku), 15 goats (āṭu) remained (niṉṟa); 15 nāḻis (?) of ghee (canay > ney) were placed (vaitta) for the sacred bath (āṭiyaruḷa) on that day (nāṉṉṟu > nāṉṟu) of Saṅkirānti falling in Uttaramayaṉam. This is under the protection of the Panmāheśvaras.
Edited and translated in , based on autopsy and photographs (by Valérie Gillet, 2016-2018).
This digital edition by Valérie Gillet and Emmanuel Francis.
